test.command: cargo test -j 4 && (cd src-tauri && cargo test -j 4) && (cd frontend && npm test -- --run)

blockingIssues.description: Out-of-scope feature change: commit fa6a52e replaced an existing startup println! with tracing::info! despite explicit feature scope stating println replacements belonged to a separate feature.

Commit ec5bc86 changed frontend duplicate result literals to duplicate_file_a/duplicate_file_b while backend still emitted duplicate_filea/duplicate_fileb at that point, creating a frontend/backend wire-contract mismatch for duplicate rows.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a documented "tooling/dependency/deletion-only" fast path to rust-worker, frontend-worker, and fullstack-worker that allows baseline-first validation instead of strict red-first test authoring when behavior contracts are intentionally unchanged.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Multiple reviews flagged procedure mismatches for this pattern: m1-drop-env-logger-adopt-tracing, m1-add-thiserror-anyhow-deps, m1-trim-tokio-features, and m1-drop-dead-code-and-glob-reexports.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Allow feature-level verification scopes to override default worker verification matrices (e.g., when a feature contract requires src-tauri tests but not fmt/clippy in that crate).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: m1-drop-env-logger-adopt-tracing review noted rust-worker default verification conflicted with explicit feature-level requirements.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Resolve contradictory guidance around startup logging migration by clarifying precedence when mission-wide conventions ("no println for logging") conflict with feature-level scope freezes.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: m1-drop-env-logger-adopt-tracing review identified conflicting directives between mission AGENTS and the feature specification.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Align commit-message guidance with accepted practice (or enforce consistently) by clarifying whether chore(...) commits are permitted for dependency/tooling features.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: m1-trim-tokio-features review found AGENTS listed feat/fix/refactor while accepted feature commits used chore(deps).

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify policy for worker edits under .factory/ when a feature explicitly requires shared-library notes, to avoid conflicting instructions.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: m1-frontend-oxlint-setup review observed conflict between AGENTS prohibition on .factory changes and feature instructions requesting a .factory/library note.

rejectedObservations.observation: Worker-base YAML parsing fallback guidance should be added immediately to library docs.
Duplicate-result wire literal inconsistency in shared docs should be applied as a shared-state correction for m1.
Repeated notes that strict TDD requirements caused justified deviations on dependency/tooling-only features.
